title: Tender British Beef Sunday Roast
description: A traditional British dish featuring slow-cooked beef, garlicky flavors, and a hint of Parmigiano-Reggiano, all wrapped in a papillote for a tender and delicious Sunday roast.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef with salt, black pepper, and thyme.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the pot, stirring to combine with the garlic.
Place the beef back in the pot, then sprinkle with Parmigiano-Reggiano.
Cover the pot with parchment paper, creating a papillote.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast for 3 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
